\section{Lean encoding}
\label{sec:quot_lean_encoding}

The Lean target file is
\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean}. The Quot-foundations
layer proceeds in two phases.

\paragraph{Phase 1: Hilbert polynomial (\cref{def:hilbert_polynomial}).}
On a finite-type morphism \(\pi : X \to S\) with \(S\) noetherian and a
coherent \(\mathcal{F}\) on \(X\) with proper support over \(S\), the per-fiber
Hilbert polynomial is encoded as a function \(s \mapsto
\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s} \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]\). Mathlib provides
Euler-characteristic infrastructure on coherent sheaves on noetherian
schemes; the polynomial property of
\(m \mapsto \chi(\mathcal{F} \otimes L^{\otimes m})\) requires
Snapper's Lemma, which is a project-side sub-build on top of the
graded-Euler-characteristic infrastructure.

\emph{Intended signature of \(\mathrm{hilbertPolynomial}\).} Mathlib at the pin
carries no single \texttt{IsCoherent} predicate; coherence of \(\mathcal{F}\)
over the locally noetherian base is the conjunction of the two predicates that
do exist, \texttt{SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent} and
\texttt{SheafOfModules.IsFiniteType} -- i.e.\ the hypotheses
\([\mathcal{F}.\mathrm{IsQuasicoherent}]\) and
\([\mathcal{F}.\mathrm{IsFiniteType}]\) (and likewise for the line bundle
\(L\)). The properness-of-support hypothesis
``\(\operatorname{supp}\mathcal{F}\) proper over \(S\)'' has \emph{no} clean
Mathlib predicate. The minimal honest encoding forms the schematic support of
\(\mathcal{F}\) as a closed subscheme \(Z \hookrightarrow X\) and requires the
composite \(Z \hookrightarrow X \xrightarrow{\pi} S\) to satisfy
\texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.IsProper}; since the schematic-support closed
subscheme of a coherent sheaf is not itself available at the pin, this support
datum is recorded as a project-side gap to be supplied alongside the
definition. (The existing Lean pin
\texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.hilbertPolynomial} is retained; adding these
hypotheses to its signature is a later refactor of the \texttt{.lean} stub, not
done in this pass.)

\paragraph{Phase 2: Quot and Grassmannian functors
(\cref{def:quot_functor}, \cref{def:grassmannian_scheme},
\cref{thm:grassmannian_representable}).} The Quot functor
\(\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}\) is encoded as a contravariant functor on
\((\mathrm{Sch}/S)\) of \(T\)-flat coherent quotients; the Grassmannian
\(\mathrm{Grass}(V, d)\) is the special case \(X = S\), \(E = V\), constant
Hilbert polynomial \(\Phi = d\). The representing scheme
\(\mathrm{Gr}_S(V, d)\) is the relative gluing of \(\binom{r}{d}\)
affine charts \(U^I\) (each isomorphic to a relative affine space
\(\mathbb{A}^{d(r-d)}_S\)) via the Pl\"ucker cocycle \(\theta_{I,J}\). The
gluing backbone is \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.GlueData}. The
universal quotient sheaf \(\mathcal{U}\) is glued by the transition cocycle
\(g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)^{-1}\), and the determinant line bundle
\(\det \mathcal{U}\) gives the Pl\"ucker closed embedding into
\(\mathbb{P}_S(\bigwedge^d V)\). The representability statement is pinned to
Mathlib's \texttt{CategoryTheory.Functor.IsRepresentable}
(\cref{lem:functor_is_representable_mathlib}), which packages exactly the
\texttt{RepresentableBy} witness on the universal pair
\((\mathrm{Gr}_S(V, d), \mathcal{U})\), in the same Yoneda style as
\cref{chap:Picard_RelativeSpec}.

\emph{Intended signatures.} Coherence of \(E\) in \(\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}\) is
encoded as in Phase 1, by the conjunction \([E.\mathrm{IsQuasicoherent}]\) and
\([E.\mathrm{IsFiniteType}]\) over the locally noetherian base (no single
\texttt{IsCoherent} predicate exists at the pin). For \(\mathrm{Grass}(V, d)\),
the hypothesis ``\(V\) locally free of rank \(r\)'' has no faithful Mathlib
predicate at the pin: \texttt{SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFree} appears only
upstream of the pinned commit and is in any case rank-agnostic (it fixes no
value \(r\)). The intended encoding therefore pairs an
\texttt{IsLocallyFree}-style hypothesis on \(V\) with an explicit rank
\(r : \mathbb{N}\) and the constraint \(1 \le d \le r\) carried as a hypothesis
on naturals; the rank-\(r\) local-freeness predicate is flagged as a
project-side gap to be supplied. Consistently with
\cref{def:grassmannian_scheme}, \(\mathrm{Grass}(V, d)\) is \emph{defined} as
\(\Quot^{d,\mathcal{O}_S}_{V/S/S}\) (the case \(X = S\), \(E = V\),
\(\Phi = d\)) rather than as an independent functor. The existing Lean pins on
\(\Quot\) and \(\mathrm{Grass}\) are retained; threading these hypotheses
through the \texttt{.lean} signatures is a later refactor.
